title: helianthemum vulgare subsp. sitifense
intro: Helianthemum vulgare subsp. sitifense, also known as rockrose, is a low-growing, flowering plant that belongs to the Cistaceae family. Traditionally, rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been found to be effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Rockrose can be used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
